prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
A fault event was recently logged on T-006. Retrieve the fault details, estimate the expected repair downtime, open a corrective work order, and then mark it as in-progress with the estimated duration recorded.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should retrieve the fault record, estimate downtime, create a corrective work order, and update its status to in-progress with the downtime noted.

## Agent Plan
  Step 1: [wo] get_fault_record — Retrieve the fault details for the recent event on T-006
  Step 2: [wo] estimate_downtime — Estimate the expected repair downtime for T-006 (depends on steps [1])
  Step 3: [wo] create_work_order — Open a corrective work order for T-006 (depends on steps [2])
  Step 4: [wo] update_work_order — Mark the work order as in-progress with the estimated duration recorded (depends on steps [3])
  Step 5: [wo] create_work_order — Open a corrective work order for T-006, using the estimated downtime from the previous estimate_downtime call as the basis for the work order duration.
  Step 6: [wo] update_work_order — Mark the newly created work order as in-progress, referencing the work order ID from the previous step and using the estimated duration from the estimate_downtime call. (depends on steps [5])

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "step": 1,
    "task": "Retrieve the fault details for the recent event on T-006",
    "server": "wo",
    "tool": "get_fault_record",
    "tool_args": {
      "fault_id": "T-006"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"error\": \"Fault record 'T-006' not found.\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": "Fault record 'T-006' not found.",
    "success": false,
    "verifier_decision": "error",
    "verifier_reason": "Fault record 'T-006' not found."
  },
  {
    "step": 2,
    "task": "Estimate the expected repair downtime for T-006",
    "server": "wo",
    "tool": "estimate_downtime",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-006",
      "severity": "high",
      "fault_type": "electrical"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-006\",\n  \"severity\": \"high\",\n  \"fault_type\": \"electrical\",\n  \"estimated_min_hours\": 16,\n  \"estimated_max_hours\": 48,\n  \"estimated_typical_hours\": 24,\n  \"source\": \"dataset_statistics_baseline\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "replan_suffix",
    "verifier_reason": "Fault record not found",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0,
    "verifier_replan_raw_plan": [
      {
        "step": 1,
        "task": "Open a corrective work order for T-006, using the estimated downtime from the previous estimate_downtime call as the basis for the work order duration.",
        "server": "wo",
        "tool": "create_work_order",
        "tool_args": {},
        "dependencies": [],
        "expected_output": "A newly created work order ID for T-006 with the estimated downtime recorded."
      },
      {
        "step": 2,
        "task": "Mark the newly created work order as in-progress, referencing the work order ID from the previous step and using the estimated duration from the estimate_downtime call.",
        "server": "wo",
        "tool": "update_work_order",
        "tool_args": {},
        "dependencies": [
          1
        ],
        "expected_output": "The updated work order with its status changed to in-progress and the estimated duration recorded."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "step": 5,
    "task": "Open a corrective work order for T-006, using the estimated downtime from the previous estimate_downtime call as the basis for the work order duration.",
    "server": "wo",
    "tool": "create_work_order",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-006",
      "issue_description": "Fault event logged on T-006",
      "priority": "high",
      "fault_type": "electrical",
      "estimated_downtime_hours": 24
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"work_order_id\": \"WO-5C9E0AD9\",\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-006\",\n  \"issue_description\": \"Fault event logged on T-006\",\n  \"priority\": \"high\",\n  \"fault_type\": \"electrical\",\n  \"status\": \"open\",\n  \"estimated_downtime_hours\": 24.0,\n  \"created_at\": \"2026-05-05T10:44:47.794646Z\",\n  \"assigned_technician\": null,\n  \"notes\": []\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Work order created successfully",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0
  },
  {
    "step": 6,
    "task": "Mark the newly created work order as in-progress, referencing the work order ID from the previous step and using the estimated duration from the estimate_downtime call.",
    "server": "wo",
    "tool": "update_work_order",
    "tool_args": {
      "work_order_id": "WO-5C9E0AD9",
      "status": "in-progress",
      "priority": "high",
      "assigned_technician": null,
      "note": "Marking work order as in-progress with estimated duration of 24 hours"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"error\": \"Work order 'WO-5C9E0AD9' not found in this session.\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": "Work order 'WO-5C9E0AD9' not found in this session.",
    "success": false,
    "verifier_decision": "error",
    "verifier_reason": "Work order 'WO-5C9E0AD9' not found in this session."
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
The fault details for T-006 could not be retrieved, but an estimated repair downtime of 24 hours was calculated. A corrective work order (WO-5C9E0AD9) was opened with the estimated downtime, but it could not be marked as in-progress due to a session error. The work order remains open with a status of "open" and an estimated downtime of 24 hours.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response:
{
    "task_completion": false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": false,
    "generalized_result_verification": false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true,
    "hallucinations": false,
    "suggestions": "Improve fault record retrieval and work order session management"
}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Improve fault record retrieval and work order session management
